title: Constrained interactive C++ function probes
summary: DansRunFunction executes an explicit C++ invocation expression for the saved function under the cursor in a generated temporary harness and shows compile/runtime output in a reusable result window.
details: The harness includes the current translation unit, renames an existing main when necessary, and evaluates the expression exactly once through a dependent callable.
Void results print <void>; streamable results use operator<<; otherwise an ADL to_string result is attempted before an explicit unprintable marker.
The shared compile-database flags are converted from compile-only to a debug link command with function/data sections and section garbage collection; extra link flags may be configured.
The result view exposes r rerun, e edit invocation, i edit stdin, h inspect the exact generated harness, and q cleanup. Bang reruns the last invocation for the same function.

Compilation and execution are explicit user actions. The tool does not claim arbitrary isolation: unresolved project libraries, global initialization, hardware state, side effects, and process environment may prevent or invalidate a probe.
C and CUDA/device probes are rejected in the current version; debugger-state capture is not part of this tool.

provenance.note: Owner requested individually runnable functions with supplied input and output or state.

evidence.claim: Generated harnesses are compiled and executed for value and void results.
The real result lifecycle, expression edit/recompile, harness inspection, and source integrity are covered.

questions: How can I run one C++ function with chosen arguments?
What can the function probe print?
Why are CUDA functions or debugger state unsupported?

last_verified.method: Real compiler/runtime integration and result-window interaction tests.
